text.title: feature-flags-types-lifecycle Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for feature-flags-types-lifecycle as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Flag debt: never removing flags after full rollout.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each untouched flag keeps a dead code branch alive, doubles the test surface, and increases cyclomatic complexity indefinitely.

failure-mode.description: Nested flags: multiple flags creating complex compound conditions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Two nested flags produce four code paths; three produce eight. Untestable in practice and impossible to reason about during incidents.

failure-mode.description: Flag dependencies: one flag depending on another being enabled.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Creates implicit ordering constraints that are not visible in the registry; changing one flag silently breaks another.

failure-mode.description: Using flags for config: storing configuration values (URLs, thresholds) in a toggle system.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Flags are binary on/off controls. Config management systems (env vars, Vault, AWS Parameter Store) are the right tool for values.

failure-mode.description: Flag names that drift across versions: new_checkout_v2, new_checkout_v3, checkout_redesign.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each rename orphans the old toggle in the registry while creating a new one; agents tasked with flag management can no longer correlate history.
